entry.title: Re: environment variables In SAP Datasphere
entry.content: Hi Witalij, I am creating a data flow from an oData service, but I should not bring 100% of the data from the source system, but only the data for this year, and so on for each new year. I was thinking of using something like ":$CURRENT_YEAR", but I haven't been able to do it. Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Reverse Tax Engine - Payroll USA
entry.content:
@CarlosAccorsi 

Hi Carlos -

Could you please check the case 84903/2025 that we have logged?  We have been back and forth on this issue for almost two weeks now and still no resolution.  I have gone over and over this blog post, read all the OSS notes, checked all of our configuration and still nothing works.

entry.title: How add custom field on XML/PDF on a eDocument for Chile?
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I need to add a custom field to the XML and PDF of an eDocument for Chile in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud. However, I haven't found a specific BAdI for Chile that allows adding custom fields.

I noticed that there is a BAdI called EDOC_MX_FILL_CUSTOM_DATA, which is used for other countries like Mexico to modify the eDocument XML before generation and another BAdI to add this field to PDF also for Mexico.  App Extensibility: eDocument Cockpit 

Would this BAdI work for Chile as well? If not, is there a General BAdI or another recommended approach to add custom fields to the eDocument XML/PDF for Chile?

I appreciate any insights or guidance you can provide.
Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: Manage KPI Report issue with dynamic passing paramete...
entry.content:
Hi, 

I believe the derivation annotation only works in combination with annotation @consumption.hidden: true.

entry.title: Re: Best practice recommendations on "Save all objects to...

entry.title: Simple Table Maintenance in SAP Fiori Using UI5 Smart Controls
entry.content:
1. What Is a Classic Maintenance View/Table in SAP GUI?

In the SAP GUI world, creating a maintenance view (or table maintenance dialog) is straightforward using transaction-based tools such as SM30. You can generate a maintenance view for a custom table, enabling easy data creation, updates, and deletions. This approach requires minimal coding—SAP generates the necessary screens, logic, and button controls automatically.

Such classic maintenance views are often used by functional teams or administrators to manage configuration tables, text tables, or other small datasets. The big advantage is that it’s a “no-frills” approach — it just works. However, as organizations move to SAP S/4HANA and Fiori, many are looking for a modern UI that is responsive, mobile-friendly, and integrated with Fiori Launchpad.

2. What Are the Current Solutions for It in Fiori with and without RAP?

When transitioning to SAP Fiori, you can no longer rely on SM30-like screens for table maintenance in a straightforward way. Instead you have workarounds like object page editing: Embed an edit-enabled table on an object page to mimic SM30. While this can work, it’s often not the most straightforward approach if your goal is simply to maintain a table of records (see blog post Generating SAP Fiori Table Maintenance Dialogs in SAP S/4HANA 2021.)

3. What Makes My Solution Better?

The solution outlined here is designed with simplicity in mind and takes advantage of the following:

  • OData V2: Avoid mandatory draft handling and simplify immediate data saves.
  • Smart Controls: Declarative UI annotations determine how fields are displayed and edited, minimizing custom coding for the UI layout.
  • Freestyle UI5 App: This approach gives you flexibility to add custom buttons, event handlers, or logic but still using the annotations from the backend

Essentially, you get a classic “table maintenance” style application but with a modern, responsive Fiori interface, plus the flexibility to extend it if your requirements grow.

4. How Does It Work? (With Code Samples)

Below is an overview of the key pieces in this project. The full sample is available on GitHub: https://github.com/marianfoo/fiori-table-maintenance.

4.1 Main View (XML)

The main view is a simple UI5 XML view containing a SmartFilterBar and a SmartTable. With the “editable” property set to true and editTogglable also enabled, the table allows inline editing. We also have buttons for refreshing and saving changes.

 

<mvc:View controllerName="com.starwars.fighter.controller.MainView"
    xmlns:mvc="sap.ui.core.mvc"
    xmlns="sap.m"
    xmlns:table="sap.ui.table"
    xmlns:f="sap.f"
    xmlns:smarttable="sap.ui.comp.smarttable"
    xmlns:smartvariants="sap.ui.comp.smartvariants"
    xmlns:smartfilterbar="sap.ui.comp.smartfilterbar"
    xmlns:app="http://schemas.sap.com/sapui5/extension/sap.ui.core.CustomData/1"
    xmlns:core="sap.ui.core">
    <Page id="page" title="{i18n>title}">
        <f:DynamicPage id="dynamicPageId" headerExpanded="true" toggleHeaderOnTitleClick="true">
            <f:title>
                <f:DynamicPageTitle>
                    <f:heading>
                        <smartvariants:SmartVariantManagement 
                            id="svm" 
                            persistencyKey="StarWarsFighterVariants"
                            showShare="true"/>
                    </f:heading>
                </f:DynamicPageTitle>
            </f:title>

            <f:header>
                <f:DynamicPageHeader pinnable="true">
                    <smartfilterbar:SmartFilterBar 
                        id="smartFilterBar" 
                        entitySet="StarWarsFighter" 
                        persistencyKey="StarWarsFighterFilter"
                        useToolbar="false">
                    </smartfilterbar:SmartFilterBar>
                </f:DynamicPageHeader>
            </f:header>

            <f:content>
                <smarttable:SmartTable 
                    id="smartTable" 
                    entitySet="StarWarsFighter" 
                    smartFilterId="smartFilterBar"
                    smartVariant="svm"
                    tableType="Table" 
                    enableExport="true"
                    editable="true"
                    editTogglable="true"
                    app:useSmartToggle="true"
                    app:useSmartField="true"
                    beforeExport=".onBeforeExport" 
                    useVariantManagement="true"
                    useTablePersonalisation="true"
                    header="Star Wars Fighters"
                    showRowCount="true"
                    enableAutoColumnWidth="true"
                    enableAutoBinding="true"
                    persistencyKey="StarWarsFighterSmartTable"
                    fieldChange=".onFieldChange"
                    class="sapUiResponsiveMargin">

                    <smarttable:customToolbar>
                        <OverflowToolbar>
                            <ToolbarSpacer />
                            <Button text="Refresh Table" type="Emphasized" press=".onRefreshTable"/>
                            <Button text="Save Changes" type="Emphasized" press=".onSubmitChanges"/>
                        </OverflowToolbar>
                    </smarttable:customToolbar>
                </smarttable:SmartTable>
            </f:content>
        </f:DynamicPage>
    </Page>
</mvc:View>

 

 

4.2 TypeScript Controller

The controller handles basic logic: refreshing the table binding and submitting changes. Notice the use of model.submitChanges() to save modifications to the server.
With the onFieldChange method everytime a user changes a field (and leaves the field or pressing enter) the changes are submitted to the backend.
You can also use the button to only send the changes to the backend when it is pressed.
The UI5 Framework is smart enough to notice only the changes and create batch requests for it.

 

import SmartTable from 'sap/ui/comp/smarttable/SmartTable';
import Controller from 'sap/ui/core/mvc/Controller';
import ODataModel from 'sap/ui/model/odata/v2/ODataModel';
import Table from 'sap/ui/table/Table';

/**
 * @namespace com.starwars.fighter.controller
 */
export default class MainView extends Controller {
  public onInit(): void {
    // Initialization logic (if needed)
  }

  public onRefreshTable(): void {
    const smartTable = this.byId('smartTable') as SmartTable;
    const innerTable = smartTable.getTable() as Table;
    innerTable.getBinding('rows').refresh();
  }

  public onFieldChange(): void {
    const smartTable = this.byId('smartTable') as SmartTable;
    const model = smartTable.getModel() as ODataModel;
    // Immediately submit changes whenever a field is changed
    model.submitChanges();
  }

  public async onSubmitChanges(): Promise {
    const smartTable = this.byId('smartTable') as SmartTable;
    const model = smartTable.getModel() as ODataModel;
    model.submitChanges();
  }
}

 

4.3 Annotations (annotations.cds)

UI Annotations let Smart Controls know which fields to display by default, which fields are editable, and how to label them. I use standard annotations like LineItems and SelectionFields.
In CAP i can use the "Computed" Annotation to make a field in the table not editable.
I made only the status field editable.

 

annotate sw.StarWarsFighter with {
    name @title: 'Fighter Name' .Computed;
    manufacturer @title: 'Manufacturer' .Computed;
);

 

4.4 CAP Schema

The CAP schema (CDS) defines the entities (StarWarsFighter and FighterStatus) and their properties. CAP automatically generates an OData service based on this definition.
With the CAP Plugin @cap-js-community/odata-v2-adapter i can automatically create the V2 Service i need in my app.

 

namespace my.starwars;

entity FighterStatus {
    key code: String;
    name: String;
    description: String;
    isOperational: Boolean;

    fighters: Association to many StarWarsFighter on fighters.status = $self;
}

entity StarWarsFighter {
    key id: String(10);
    name: String;
    manufacturer: String;
    model: String;
    fighterCategory: String;
    builtAt: Timestamp;
    height: Decimal(5,2);
    weight: Decimal(7,2);
    length: Decimal(5,2);
    width: Decimal(5,2);
    crewSize: Integer;
    passengerCapacity: Integer;
    propulsion: String;
    maxAtmosphericSpeed: String;
    maxAcceleration: String;
    hyperdriveClass: String;
    laserCannons: Integer;
    protonTorpedoCapacity: Integer;
    additionalWeapons: String;

    status: Association to FighterStatus;
}

 

 

4.5 Sample Data

Finally, you can load some sample CSV data into the StarWarsFighter entity. Here’s a snippet:

 

id,name,manufacturer,model,fighterCategory,builtAt,height,weight,length,width,crewSize,passengerCapacity,propulsion,maxAtmosphericSpeed,maxAcceleration,hyperdriveClass,laserCannons,protonTorpedoCapacity,additionalWeapons,status_code
SF001,X-wing,Incom Corporation,T-65B,Assault,2020-01-15T10:00:00Z,2.4,10000,12.5,11.76,1,0,Twin ion engines,1050 km/h,3100 G,1.0 HCR,4,6,Deflector shields,AVAILABLE
SF002,TIE Fighter,Sienar Fleet Systems,TIE/ln,Interceptor,2020-02-20T08:30:00Z,7.5,6300,6.3,6.4,1,0,Twin ion engines,1200 km/h,4100 G,0,2,0,None,MISSION
...

 

With all these parts combined, you have a full CAP-based, OData V2 service that your UI5 application (featuring SmartTable) can consume. The end result is a modern UI for table maintenance with immediate saving, filter capabilities, sorting, and variant management—similar to the classic SM30 experience but Fiori-style.

Conclusion

Switching from the “classic” SAP GUI table maintenance to a Fiori-based solution doesn’t have to be complex. By using CAP/RAP Backend, a freestyle UI5 app, and OData V2 (plus UI annotations), you can quickly set up a user-friendly, fully responsive table maintenance application. You get instant saves without the overhead of drafts, plus the flexibility to adapt the UI to more complex scenarios if needed.

Check out the full source and try it out yourself on GitHub: https://github.com/marianfoo/fiori-table-maintenance.

Feel free to leave a comment or ask questions below if you have any feedback or want to share your experiences with table maintenance in Fiori!

entry.title: SAP Global Hierarchy activation impact on existing hierarchies
entry.content:
We are looking to leverage Global Hierarchy Functionality to import GL hierarchy from a different system into SAP. GL Hierarchies maintain in the third-party application is used for cost rollup and business wants us to import those into SAP for cost reports in SAP. 

We are looking to leverage the Global Hierarchy functionality as its easier to maintain and manage. Is there an impact on existing FSV Hierarchies if we activate #GlobalHierarchies . Meaning, can we have both existing FSV (old SAP hierarchy functionality) and new GL Hierarchy via Global Hierarchy concurrently/same time. 

Or do we have to mandatorily convert old FSV hierarchies to new ones? Please advise. 

Appreciate all the help and responses.

entry.title: [ODataMetadata] initial loading of metadata failed - execute locally from VS code
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I developed a Fiori application in Visual Studio code and deployed to SAP system, configured in Fiori launchpad where I am able to run it successfully.

But the same application when executed locally from VS code (http://localhost:8080/index.html?sap-client=400&sap-ui-xx-viewCache=false), I am getting the below error. 

I was not able to figure out what I am missing. Maybe some proxy setup or something? Please let me know. Appreciate your response.

Thank you in advance!

entry.title: Re: Prevent Occurence of list header and page breaks in spool file of ALV Grid
entry.content: Please see https://me.sap.com/notes/2861244. It is required that you set print parameter 'Spool List with Structure Information' for the job step.

entry.title: Stay certified - Is an SAP Learning Hub subscription required for accessing new learning journeys?
entry.content:
Hi!

I received a notification recently that the C_S4CPB Implementation Consultant—SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition will expire in 8 weeks, and now I am supposed to take the assessment to extend the validity of my certification. However, my SAP Learning Hub subscription has expired, and I only see old learning materials for which I passed the assessment last year (for Cloud version 2402), not the new ones for version 2502. I know that taking the assessment requires an active SAP Learning Hub subscription, but does access to the latest learning journey also require a Learning Hub subscription?

Thanks for any help on this topic.

Mihael

entry.title: Re: how to get spool file from SAP (not using SM37)
entry.content: SP01 is the transaction you can use to view all spool requests. With report BTCAUX05, you can check by which jobs spool requests have been created.

entry.title: Re: Which interpretation algorithm is used to extract the...
entry.content:
Hi,

 

yes so what you are looking for are the interpretation algorithms. You find the overview here: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/3cb1182b4a184bdd93f8d62e3f1f0741/4dc5efb40c381c36e10000000a42189b.html

 

In the old world you defined a posting rule for a specific external transaction, which would apply the algorithm which you have maintained in the rule. 

In the new world, you create a processing rule, which has action Type "Clear in Sequence" which applies a processing instruction (which does the same in the end, depending on the algorithm behind it)

 

More information here: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/3cb1182b4a184bdd93f8d62e3f1f0741/ed31be4969ce485085da152c3a3e8062.html

 

Hope this explains everything. 

Best,

Edrilan Berisha

entry.title: Re: SAP Desktop Agent is not running in unattended mode
entry.content:
What is the trigger configuration that you have done for the automation.

Please share a screenshot for the same.

Right now, based on the details shared by you, doesn't look like a scheduled/api based trigger has been created for the automation.

entry.title: Re: How can I extract the reference number from the <Ustr...
entry.content:
Hi,

 

what you try to do is currently not possible. But we work on something which might help in the future. So stay tuned and read the What's News.

Now for your case, usually you would simply map the data which the bank sends to these fields 1:1. Then you execute some of the interpretation algorithms, which would extract the reference number from the Note to Payee field. 

 

Best,

Edrilan Berisha

entry.title: Re: sap gui gesperrt nach mehreren Fehlversuchen sich anzumelden
entry.content:
Hallo,

ich habe es so versucht wie Sie beschrieben haben, aber bei mir kommt noch immer der Fehlermeldung

"Kennwortanmeldung nicht mehr möglich- zu viele Fehlversuche", Ich bitte um Hilfe wie ich mich hier anmelden könnte.

Vielen Dank!

entry.title: Re: Unable to find SubTotal function in SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content: I could not find the subtotal function either, only the grandtotal function is available.

entry.title: Re: From User Exits to RAP: Adopting a Clean Core Approach in S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hi, did you create the event handler class under a Standard ABAP package or ABAP Cloud Package? Is it still considered as clean core even though we created the class under the Standard ABAP package? 

@Andre_Fischer , any thoughts?

entry.title: Re: SAC Live Connection Schedule Publication not working
entry.content: As schedule publication on live connection requires 'Advance feature' setting in connection or tunnel connection, probably it worth to check the connection setting as well as the cloud connector.

entry.title: Re: Effective date when status is "Inactive" for a Depart...
entry.content:
Hi R,

Could you please try to add a custom field (end date) on object Department? And configure a rule like: if jobInfo.department.value.enddate is equal to date plus () (today plus certain days) , then raise message.

Regards,

Kayla

entry.title: Activity Rate planning
entry.content:
Dear PERSON_NAME,
Could you please explain how to manage activity rates in planning concerning cost centers in the SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Fiori app? We have checked the Fiori Reference Library but could not find any relevant app.
In other private cloud or ECC systems, we typically use transaction codes KP26 and KBK6 to perform this task.
Looking forward to your guidance.
 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Activity Rate planning
entry.content:
Kindly use below apps for cost rate planning, see

Manage Cost Rates - Plan

Manage Cost Rates - Services

Hope above information helps.

entry.title: Re: Difference between job type "Synch HRIS data" and "HR...
entry.content:
Hi,

There is not much difference between the two job types. Sync HRIS Data job is scheduled in Scheduled Job Manager in the tenant while HRIS Sync job is scheduled in provisioning. And there's more Job Parameters can be configured in HRIS Sync job in the provisioning. For example, Compensation specific options for HRIS sync job, Sync job runtime options. Hope this clarifies. 

Regards,

Kayla

entry.title: Re: SuccessFactors EC. Position Management change manager...
entry.content: Hi, you can update position->department via job info to position sync.

entry.title: Re: SuccessFactors EC. Position Management change manager...
entry.content: Hi, you can update position->department via job info to position sync but manager change in job info cannot be updated to position by changing the parent position. This can only be done in the opposition direction. Namely, update manager based on parent position change via position to job info sync.

entry.title: Re: Access Denied on project in SAP Build Apps
entry.content: Hi, I have similar issues for past 2 days. could you please check on this?

entry.title: Re: User Info portlet in the Latest People Profile
entry.content:
Hi,

Unfortunately, USERINFO_BLOCK is not migrated to Latest People Profile. Please refer to the below content:

 https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3529276#:~:text=Attempt%20to%20add%20this%20standard%20block%20in%20the,The%20USERINFO_BLOCK%20is%20mainly%20for%20non-Employee%20Central%20customers.

Best regards,

Elsa

entry.title: Re: From User Exits to RAP: Adopting a Clean Core Approach in S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hi @RavdeepSingh ,

Whether this event will trigger via GUI t-code(va01) or it is not possible for GUI t-code.

Whether this event will trigger only when we use the respective RAP based fiori app where the Event is declared 

Please suggest.

Thanks

entry.title: Facing below error Inbound processing in endpoint at /EC_to_ERP_Generic_Object_Query
entry.content:
Hi Expert,

I am facing below error in Standard Replication iFlow in SAP CPI.

Inbound processing in endpoint at /EC_to_ERP_Generic_Object_Query failed with message "NullPointerException:while trying to invoke the method org.apache.catalina.TomcatPrincipal.getUserPrincipal() of a null object loaded from field org.apache.catalina.connector.Request.userPrincipal of an object loaded from local variable 'this'"

Please advice on the root cause and any steps need to be taken?

entry.title: Re: SuccessFactors EC. Position Management change manager...
entry.content:
I did have the same question as you. I get the answer after reading
1. 2636185 - Move Position to New Supervisor on Job Info Change - EC
2. 2598479 - Job to Position Sync Doesn't Synchronize Manager and Field Values in Same Transaction

However, it is annoying that either a manager change or another field data change is only effective if both happen in the same transaction.

So it can't fully meet your requirements. Finally, I use the integration center to enhance job info and position sync.

entry.title: Re: From User Exits to RAP: Adopting a Clean Core Approach in S/4HANA
entry.content: I think it will trigger from both. You can do a where-used of the event in the RAP BO. Another way to check is to implement the event handler class and maybe have a breakpoint.

entry.title: Re: Consuming a released event of an unreleased RAP BO (h...
entry.content: Hi, there is an event for our requirement. Our question is whether consuming these events locally is classified as Tier 1. Currently, to consume the events, we need to create the class under the Standard ABAP package in S4H 2023 PCE.

entry.title: Re: Advanced Intercompany Return Not inspected
entry.content: Hi, have you solved this problem? I've meet the same problem.

entry.title: Re: Mass Data Updates from Excel in UI5 and Fiori elements with this Open Source Project

Hi Marian,

This is great and works like a charm. RAP excel upload for data maintenance had been missing something like this. I do have a question around the component usage though, do you think we will have to specify a version every time as I see on github now there is a V1_7_3 already.

v1_7_2

Thanks,

Rahul

entry.title: Re: EWM Layout-Oriented Storage Control (LOSC) – A simple guide

entry.title: Hello, May we know if the reported issue is fixed or still persists ?
entry.content: Hello, May we know if the reported issue is fixed or still persists ?

entry.title: Re: Accessing MIGO Through Fiori Launchpad on Mobile Device
entry.content: There should be no problem with accessing MIGO from a mobile device via FLP. Can you access it when using a desktop browser? In other words, is the problem specific to using a phone/tablet?

entry.title: Re: /SCWM/TO_CREATE_WHR Create Putaway Warehouse Task for...
entry.content:
Hi Florian, Thanks for your reply.

Yes, the Storage Type is mean to be only have 1 HU. 

for my case which 1 delivery item has 2 HU, i did Loop the HU, then for each HU i assigned to the different Warehouse Type, and Storage Bin. which I did have 1 internal table with 2 lines.

  • Both line have same information on DOCCAT, DOCID, ITEMID from inbound delivery
  • Each line have each unique HU, S. Type, S. Bin.

 to be more clear, below is the code inside the loop

 

LOOP AT lt_delivery_hu_data INTO ls_delivery. 
   APPEND INITIAL LINE TO lt_create_putaway_single ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_create_whr_putaway>).
   <ls_create_whr_putaway>-rdoccat = ls_delviery-doccat. "(Inbound Delivery Doc Cat)
   <ls_create_whr_putaway>-rdocid  = ls_delivery-docid. "(Inbound Delivery Doc ID)
   <ls_create_whr_putaway>-ritmid  = ls_delivery-itemid. "(Inbound Delivery Item ID)

   <ls_create_whr_putaway>-nlpla   = ls_delivery-lgpla.   "(Target S.Bin)
   <ls_create_whr_putaway>-nltyp   = ls_delivery-lgtyp.   "(Target S.Type)
   <ls_create_whr_putaway>-nlber  ​ = ls_delivery-huident. "(Target HU)

   CALL FUNCTION '/SCWM/TO_CREATE_WHR'
     EXPORTING
       iv_lgnum       = is_data_to_process-lgnum
       iv_process     = wmegc_whr_proc_pu
       iv_to_init     = 'X'
       iv_bname       = sy-uname
       it_create_whr  = lt_create_putaway_single
       iv_update_task = ' '
       iv_commit_work = lv_commit
     IMPORTING
        ev_tanum       = lv_wt_result
        et_ltap_vb     = lt_ltap_vb
        et_bapiret     = lt_bapiret
        ev_severity    = lv_severity
ENDLOOP.

 

My aim is to create a single putaway warehouse task for each HU, is that the correct approach?
or Should I remove the Target S.Bin, S. Type and HU from the parameters so the system will determine itself?

entry.title: Google Play: APK REQUIRES PROMINENT DISCLOSURE
entry.content:
Hi All
My app has been removed from Google Play because the app is not compliant with the User Data policy because:

  • Your app is uploading users' Image information without a prominent disclosure.

First up, I am not deliberately uploading any image information...any data at all for that matter.

Can such settings be controlled?  I recall in earlier versions of Build Apps, at the build stage, I could select certain options similar to this but that doesn't seem the case anymore.

Generally, has anyone come across this and any guidance would be much appreciated.

Edit- I suspect this relates to permissions in the manifest, so is it possible to edit these?

Thanks

entry.title: Any solutions ?
entry.content: #pmgm# delegation

entry.title: BKP - Customer Returns Product Valuation with Reference to Sales
entry.content:
In S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Customer Returns Solution (Scope Item BKP) can handle different returns scenarios in an effective way.  Product valuation and sales cost reverse in returns logistics are important.

Business Context

Before CE2502, in customer returns, product valuation and sales cost reverse can only use the inventory price from product master record at time when the returned product is valuated.

This is not flexible enough as in certain countries or regions, the returned product valuation and sales cost reversal should be consistent with the price in sales. This reflects the legal requirements.

In CE2502, a new process variant ‘Valuate with reference’ is enriched for customer returns solution. With this variant, additionally, product valuation and sales cost reversal in customer returns can refer to the price in sales process. This ensures that the resulting journal entry reflects the actual product value and cost reversal.

Innovation details

Returned product valuation with reference can be used for sales process where a real goods issue takes place, such as sells from stock (anonymous stock), or sales scenario where special stock type, like sales order stock is used. In this demonstration, sales process with scope item BDN - Sales of Non-Stock Item with Order-Specific Procurement is used.

Sales of Non-Stock Item with Order-Specific Procurement

In sales process, 100 PC of product TG14 are procured and received into the special stock – sales order stock. Later, 100 PC of product TG14 are issued from sales order stock.  Checking the journal entry for good issue, the price used for inventory account and sales cost is 1000USD / 100PC = 10 USD/PC.

Customer Returns Product Valuation without reference

In such case, returned product valuation and sales cost reversal are using the price from product master data as default.

The product TG14 under plant 1710 has the inventory price 9.79 USD/PC (Price Control – Moving average price 9.79 USD/PC) for fiscal period when customer returns product valuation happens.

Create a returns order with reference to sales order with Manage Customer Returns Version 2 App,

If the central switch ‘Valuation with reference to Sales’ is not active, or ‘Valuate with reference’ variant is not selected in returns order item, returned product valuation and sales cost reversal are using the price from product master data.

In such case, 1 PC of product TG14 is returned into stock and valuated.  Inventory account is debited with 9.79 USD, and Sales Cost account is credited with 9.79 USD.   The price here is from product master data.

Customer Returns Product Valuation with reference

Create a returns order with reference to sales order with Manage Customer Returns Version 2 App,

‘Valuate with Reference’ variant is selected in returns order item.

In such case, 1 PC of product TG14 is returned into stock and valuated.  Inventory account is debited with 10 USD, and Sales Cost account is credited with 10 USD.   The price here is consistent with the price used in posting goods issue in sales. 

Configuration

By default, ‘Valuation with Reference to Sales’ is inactive in public cloud. The process variant ‘Valuate with Reference’ is not visible in returns order item when you create returns order. 

If you want to activate this process variant, you can enable it with the central switch “Valuation with Reference to Sales” via the configuration activity 106396 Configure Returns Order Type for Standard Return.

Meanwhile, you can utilize the configuration activity 106585 Define Default Values for Customer Returns Apps to set default values for different process variants. This could accelerate the user input in the Apps for creating customer returns. With this configuration activity, parameter ‘Valuate with Reference’ is available to set default value.

 

Related Documents

Customer Returns at help portal

Customer Returns (BKP)

entry.title: Show a default empty value for category drop list on the goal plan
entry.content:
Show a default empty value for category drop list on the goal plan

 

SAP SuccessFactors Performance & Goals

entry.title: Re: SAP Global Hierarchy activation impact on existing hi...
entry.content:
Global Hierarchies and Financial Statement Versions (FSV) can coexist in SAP S/4HANA. Here are the key points addressing your concerns:  and please also check the hyperlinks for details

  1. Impact on Existing FSV Hierarchies:

    • Activating Global Hierarchies does not automatically remove or replace existing FSV hierarchies [1].
    • Both set-based (old) and runtime (new) Financial Statement Hierarchies are available in several FIORI reports [2].

  2. Conversion of Old FSV Hierarchies:

    • It is not mandatory to convert old FSV hierarchies to new ones immediately [1].
    • However, there are options to migrate existing FSVs to Global Hierarchies: a. Import existing FSVs into the new architecture using the "Import Hierarchy" function in the Global Accounting Hierarchies app (Fiori App ID F2918) [1][2]. b. Use the new app "Migrate Financial Statement Versions to Global Hierarchies" introduced in SAP S/4HANA 2021 [3].

  3. Concurrent Usage:

    • You can have both existing FSV (old SAP hierarchy functionality) and new GL Hierarchy via Global Hierarchy concurrently [1][2].
    • However, it's recommended that once a hierarchy is imported into FIORI, future maintenance should be done in the FIORI app F2918 [2].

  4. Future Considerations:

    • While there's no immediate plan for deprecation, the feature strategy is moving towards Global Hierarchies for a similar experience [4].
    • SAP is clear that time is required for adoption of FSV in Hierarchies [4].

  5. Benefits of Global Hierarchies:

    • Unified way to maintain hierarchies for different master data objects [1].
    • Single dashboard to control hierarchies [1].
    • Time-dependent hierarchies, allowing creation of future hierarchies in advance [1].
    • Status control, enabling activation of hierarchies only when complete [1].

  6. Migration Process:

    • When migrating FSVs to Global Hierarchies, the FSVs can only be edited in Manage Global Hierarchies after migration [3].
    • For most hierarchy types, including FSVs, you can import existing hierarchies rather than creating them afresh in the F2918 app [2].

In conclusion, you can leverage Global Hierarchy functionality to import GL hierarchies from a different system into SAP without immediately impacting existing FSV Hierarchies. Both can coexist, allowing for a gradual transition as needed.

entry.title: Re: Google Play: APK REQUIRES PROMINENT DISCLOSURE
entry.content:
Hi there,

To address your app removal from Google Play, check your manifest file to ensure no unnecessary permissions are requested. Make sure your privacy policy is clear and prominently displayed, and update the Data Safety form in the Play iTero Console. Consider reaching out to Google Play Developer Support if needed. This may help resolve the issue.

Thanks!

entry.title: Passing variable/parameter/prompt values through URL
entry.content:
I am facing difficulty in passing the Variable/parameters/prompt values dynamically(that show up once you open a story) from Story1 to Story2. Story1 has 3 mandatory date Variable/parameters/prompts and 2 non mandatory Variable/parameters/prompts. Also Story2 has 3 mandatory date Variable/parameters/prompts and 2 non mandatory Variable/parameters/prompts.

I have a table widget in Story1 and i am adding hyperlink to the table using 'External URL' option. After applying this option a hyperlink will be shown below the table title something like "Navigate to External URL' (see attached image). Now when the user clicks on that hyperlink Story2 should open with the Variable/parameters/prompt values applied to the story1.

I created URL like 

https://<TENANT>/sap/fpa/ui/tenants/<TENANT_ID>/app.html#/story2&/s2/<STORY_ID>?v01Model=<Model_ID>&v01Par=<technical name of parameter1 in story2>&v01Val=<technical name of parameter1 in story1>&v02Par=<technical name of parameter2 in story2>&v02Val=<technical name of parameter2 in story1> ... &v05Par=<technical name of parameter5 in story2>&v05Val=<technical name of parameter5 in story1>

But this is not working. I mean story2 variable/parameter/prompt values are not updated with the variable/parameter/prompt values of the story1.

Models used in both story’s are Datasphere models

How to achieve this. Pls suggest.

entry.title: Re: Requirement of 4 savings plan and employer contributi...
entry.content:
Hi Rahul,

Currently the multiple funds are not supported in Workplace pension. 

As a workaround, you can try a business rule on save of each pension benefit that you configure. The business rule should be configured to read the look up object 'Benefit Enrollment' and check if user has already enrolled into other pension benefits and what is the employer contribution saved in that benefit. You can throw an error message on save of enrollment if the sum of Employer contirbution of all pension benefits exceed 10%.

This rule should be on save of Benefit enrollment object, and in the rule you can mention the benefit names for which it needs to be executed and read the employer contributions.

I am not sure if you are using workflows for Benefit enrollment, because if the enrollment is in pending then this rule cannot validate the Employer contribution information from Benefit enrollment object, as it only retrieves the active or approved enrollments data.

Regards,

Bijo

entry.title: Re: Restrict compensation worksheet template for specific...
entry.content:
Hi @rezasaputro ,

Yes you can restrict access to specific templates via RBP.  You will need to enable 'Enable Role-Based Permissions for Compensation and Variable Pay Template Access' within Actions for all Plans>Company Settings.  You can then see this as an option in RBP.  There are some screenshots in this KBA. 3151227 - Newly Created Template is Missing in Compensation Home - SAP for Me

Hope that helps,

Christian

entry.title: How to download PDF from from Backend S4 system
entry.content:
Hi Guru, 

Did someone has the similar requirement on PDF download? We use SAP build app for mobile UI development, the data part is provided by backend S4 HANA system. We have one Odata service to provide PDF document. 

The odata service returns content-type application/pdf

The odata service can be found in the SAP Build Integration. When we made Restful API testing run, the odata service returns text string. When i pass to Proces Download Base64 text as File (web only), it is not working. 

The HTTP request doesn't give any content back. I wonder how people to get the PDF document from the backend SAP. The PDF has been generated at the backend SAP system, we don't use any API to generate PDF dynamically, we just want to pass the PDF from backend to the front end.

 

thanks

 

John ZHANG

entry.title: How to Use Different Production Version for External Procurement and In-House Production in MRP?
entry.content:
Hello,

I'm currently using 'Both Procurement Types' for a specific semi-finished material, where the material is sometimes procured externally and sometimes produced in-house.

However, the BOM differs depending on the procurement type:

  • For External Procurement, the material follows one BOM structure.
  • For In-House Production, the material follows a different BOM structure (Alternative: 02).

To handle this, I have created alternative BOMs (two versions of BOM) and also defined two production versions.

Now, when MRP generates a planned order for this material:

  • If the planned order is converted into a purchase requisition, I want to use Production Version 001.
  • If the planned order is converted into a production order, I want to use Production Version 002.

Could anyone guide me on how to configure this in the system so that the correct production version is selected based on the procurement type?

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Mass Data Updates from Excel in UI5 and Fiori elements with this Open Source Project

@rahullohia yes, you have to specify the version you´re using, this is a feature, not a bug. And i just released a bug fix yesterday to a new version (changelog)

It is to better control which version you´re using. This is aimed at more to a ABAP Stack Deployment.
I wrote a blog post about it here: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/create-a-ui5-custom-library-with-versioning-using-a-multi-version-namespace/ba-p/13567207 

Also, thank you for using my component. Any suggestions or feedback is welcome!

Request your insight on this possibility.

entry.title: Re: Limitations in Capacity Planning in SAP S/4HANA Publi...
entry.content:
In SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition, traditional capacity planning transactions like CM25 and CM27
are replaced by Fiori applications such as Manage Work Center Capacity, Capacity Scheduling Table, and Capacity Scheduling Board.
These tools offer modern interfaces and functionalities to handle various capacity planning scenarios.

1. Handling Large Volumes of Production Orders:

When managing numerous short-duration production orders, it's essential to efficiently schedule and adjust them as needed.
The Capacity Scheduling Table app is particularly useful in this context. It provides a user-friendly interface that allows planners
to dispatch and deallocate bottleneck operations of orders transparently and easily. This tool facilitates the realignment of operations,
enabling planners to shift orders left or right to optimize the schedule and address overload situations.

2. Managing Multiple Capacities in a Work Center:

In scenarios where operations need to be distributed across multiple capacities within a work center,
the Manage Work Center Capacity app offers a clear visualization of both capacities and loads. This visibility allows planners to
detect overload situations and make informed decisions to balance the load effectively. While the app doesn't directly adjust the planned finish date,
it provides the necessary insights to ensure that Available-to-Promise (ATP) dates reflect accurate availability based on current capacity allocations.
c

3. Integration with HR Work Schedules:

Currently, SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition, does not support direct integration with HR work schedules for capacity availability.
However, planners can manually adjust work center capacities to account for employee absences such as sick leave or vacation. By proactively
managing these adjustments, planners can ensure that capacity planning remains accurate and reflects the actual availability of resources.

For a practical demonstration of managing work center capacities, you might find the following video helpful:

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=12HlcLFeI94

entry.title: Re: AI Assisted Skills Architecture Creation
entry.content:
Hi @Marcos_Ramos ,

1. This helps in access control. Do you face any problems accessing this link ? 

2. The texts will be inferred based on multiple combinations. Even if it is single word or a text, the AI inference will kick in. And yes, the translations will be available if you have activated the supported languages, as shared above in the blog.

Best Regards,

Sharath

entry.title: Re: How to Use Different Production Version for External...
entry.content:
To ensure the correct production version is selected based on the procurement type in MRP, follow these steps:

1. Maintain Production Versions: In transaction C223 (or in the "Manage Production Versions" app in Fiori), define two production versions:
001 → For External Procurement
002 → For InHouse Production

2. Set Procurement Type in Material Master:
In MM02, under the MRP 2 view, set Procurement Type = Both (E & F).

3. Assign Production Versions in the Source List (ME01):
Define a source list entry for external procurement and assign Production Version 001.

4. Maintain Quota Arrangement (MEQ1 if required):
Assign the correct production version based on the procurement percentage for planned orders.

5. Check MRP Strategy & BOM Selection:
Ensure the correct lotsizing and BOM selection are maintained in the production version settings.

This setup ensures that when converting a planned order to a purchase requisition,
Production Version 001 is used, and when converting to a production order, Production Version 002 is applied.

entry.title: How to cancel the customer contract invoice request
entry.content:
Hi,
I have a customer contract that was canceled by a user but they did not cancel the invoice request associated with this contract.
Can you help cancel the customer invoice invoice request?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to cancel the customer contract invoice request
entry.content:
The reason the Customer Contract Invoice Request is showing with the status To Be Invoiced, is because you have selected the Keep Invoice Requests in the Invoice Cancellation field of the Contract. 

As the Cancellation of the Item has already been completed, you are no longer able to change the Invoice Cancellation to Cancel Pending Invoice Requests. Also, since there is no Invoice Schedule maintained and the items are directly Invoiced via the Customer Contract Invoice Request, the invoice request cannot be cancelled anymore.

Please see below the behavior between Keep Invoice Requests and Cancel Pending Invoice Requests.

Keep Invoice Requests: The system will not cancel any Invoice Requests.
Cancel Pending Invoice Requests: System will cancel any Invoice Requests that are in the status To Be Invoiced.

 

Since the customer contract is completed, no any further changes is allowed. To close the invoice request, you can invoice the Customer Contract Invoice Request with a 0 Amount.

see KBA: 3388525 - Cannot Cancel Customer Contract Invoice Request

entry.title: Re: DNS configuration details for Onboarding Form in SAP...
entry.content:
1. DNS Configuration & Subdomains: DNS is used to resolve domain names for SAP S/4HANA RISE. A subdomain organizes network traffic,
and primary/secondary DNS ensures redundancy.
2. Existing vs. New DNS: You can use the existing DNS details in the COF and update them later when the new domain is ready.
Inform SAP support to facilitate a smooth transition.
3. S2D Document Requirement: If S2D details are mandatory but unavailable, consult SAP support for alternative documentation options.
Reference Guide: [Pre-provisioning Best Practices for RISE with SAP](https://nttdata-solutions.com/us/blog/pre-provisioning-for-a-seamless-rise-with-sap-deployment/)

entry.title: Re: what does "fra3" in the FQDN stand for?
entry.content:
"fra3" in the FQDN likely refers to an SAP data center region or availability zone, not necessarily the city Frankfurt.
SAP uses such codes to denote specific infrastructure zones within cloud provider regions (e.g., Azure, AWS).

"fra" typically represents Frankfurt, but "fra3" could be an internal SAP designation for a specific cluster or network segment.
The presence of "fra3" in Amsterdam suggests it may be a logical assignment rather than a strict geographical reference.
"rot" likely refers to SAP’s data center in St. LeonRot, Germany.

entry.title: MRP Run not considering firm PRs and POs and creating duplicate PRs
entry.content:
I have a scenario where there are 2 plants X and Y. When MRP is run in plant X, it checks for stock in plant Y due to the special procurement key and creates an external PR if the material is not available in Y as well. Now consider, PR1 is generated in plant Y, we change it manually and point it to plant X and the PR becomes firm. 

When we run the MRP again in plant X, even with the existence of a firm PR, or even if we convert it to PO, it does not consider their presence and creates a duplicate PR for the same qty in plant X for plant Y.

How do I ensure MRP run considers firm PRs and POs when rerun is executed?

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Apps Authorization issues
entry.content: hi, is by deleting and creating a new sub-account the only way to do this? im having the same issue as well right now. i can access the lobby however cannot open any of my projects

entry.title: Re: How to show the Story Filter value in a Textbox in SAC?
entry.content:
Hello,

I wanted to say, that you can use a script for that, but it seems that the getdimensionfilter on the story filter is full buggy.

As workaround, you can place an inputcontrol with same dimension as the story filter and you use the inputcontrol as dynamic test link. For me it's working. And then you hide the input control.

I hope this works for you too

 

cya

entry.title: Re: AI Assisted Skills Architecture Creation
entry.content: It takes you to a sharepoint login page, not accessible to watch this video, which sounds really interesting & helpful.

entry.title: Re: Routing: Alternative Sequence & Parallel Sequence in SAP
entry.content:
Hi, very nice article.

In the same context, I was wondering if anybody ever faced the following scenario and found a solution. We have some workcenters that can be used to split the work. i.e. When at operation 200, I have 2 workcenters that can do the same work, which means the lead time of operation 200 can be half. 

I know, standard SAP advice is to increase the capactity of the workcenter, but the issue is that these workcenters are also used separately in other routings. So I need to be able to plan them individually. 

We are working with production orders with big quantities, so for planning, this split over 2 workcenters can have a big influence on the lead time of the production order, but also on the load and availability of both workcenters. 

I would appreciate if somebody could maybe share experiences on this. 

 

kr

entry.title: Re: How to mass upload from a spreadsheet file into an SAP Fiori app in SAP BTP ABAP Environment Par
entry.content: @ngocle—Thank you for your comment. If I understand you correctly, you want to export the records displayed in the list report. If so, you can do that using the standard export feature of the FIORI Elements. This allows you to download the record in a spreadsheet. Just for this use case, I don't think any custom implementation is required. However, if you have some specific custom requirement, which can not be achieved through the standard feature, you can utilize the downloadFile RAP action and introduce a new button 'Download' on the list report table header to invoke the 'downloadFile' RAP action. This will internally retrieve all the records of the RAP BO based on the filter criteria and utilize XLSX write API to download the records in a spreadsheet.

entry.title: Inquiry about MRP plan order generation divided into production version
entry.content:
Hello, I am posting because I have an MRP question about the production version.

One material code uses two production versions. PIR established requirements using two production versions on a daily basis.
After the MRP was executed, the plan order was issued as one production order by grouping it based on the date.

 

As we established the PIR, a plan should be established for each production version, so if you know how, please reply.

It's essential, so if you know it, please answer it.
Thank you.

entry.title: SAP analytics cloud integration with Saviynt (Import users and Teams)
entry.content:
Hi,

I have configured the REST connection to connect to SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC)to create and sync data between Saviynt and SAC.

I am able to create users in SAC from Saviynt, but when I try to pull users from SAC to saviynt I get 200 success response but DENY messages in cors

SameSite=None, x-content-type-options: nosniff, x-frame-options: DENY, x-vcap-request-id: a32d2XXf-d7fe-XXX33-6XXXXXX, x-xss-protection: 1; mode=block, transfer-encoding: chunked, strict-transport-security: max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains; preload;

 

Anyone please let me know if any config to be checked. 

 

Thanks,

Praveen
